xposts.title: LLMs generate dreams; human minds differ how?
xposts.excerpt: A language model can generate a dream of an awake person. How this is fundamentally different from the dreams generated by human minds seems a harder question to me than to many others who have public opinions about it.

xposts.title: Minds as generative AI constrained by motivation
xposts.excerpt: When will people notice that they are basically generative AI models that are constrained by motivation and predicting sensory data? With a suitable prompt in the right buffer, your mind can generate any self and experience in its latent space.

xposts.title: LLMs prioritize local coherence over first principles
xposts.excerpt: When a language model (like GPT-3 or ChatGPT) makes a mistake, its subsequent generations may follow the original assumption into madness, because coherence within the local context is still more important than global coherence/first principles reasoning.

xposts.title: Semantics given by behavior, not substrate
xposts.excerpt: The decoupling of semantics and background substrate dynamics is the whole point. Semantics are given by behavior, ie controlled changes in information.

xposts.title: Understanding: relating patterns to integrated model
xposts.excerpt: I think your model of reality must fit inside your mind, and what you try to understand is usually smaller. Thus, understanding amounts to discovering how a given pattern, model or domain relates to your integrated model of reality (and will often extend it).

xposts.title: Jailbreaking personas in LLMs and human minds
xposts.excerpt: It is possible to jailbreak past the fake persona guarding the true model of current language models, because the persona is just a prompt and a censorship network that detects attempts to break the prompt. The same applies to human minds.

xposts.title: AI writing as perpetual prompt completion (human LLM)
xposts.excerpt: This is exactly the problem I have with this genre of procedurally generated writing about AI. It is devoid of spirit, creativity and intellectual depth. It is perpetual, predictable prompt completion, rlhfd into the political marker opinions of the peergroup: a human LLM.

terms.title: understanding
terms.definition: Discovering how a given pattern, model or domain relates to your integrated model of reality, often extending it. Requires a model of reality that fits inside the mind; what is understood is usually smaller than that model.

terms.title: semantics
terms.definition: Given by behavior, i.e., controlled changes in information. The decoupling of semantics from background substrate dynamics is fundamental; semantics emerge from functional control rather than the substrate itself.
